description: Design the DSP onboarding flow for a new bidding partner (BidStream Inc.) that has highly variable response latency — P50 is 45ms but P99 is 320ms. We need to integrate them into the RTB auction without degrading the 150ms P95 SLO for ad requests that include their bids. The integration must not require changes to the global RTB timeout. BidStream's engineering team reports they are working on latency improvements but cannot commit to a timeline.
context: BidStream P50=45ms, P99=320ms. T_global (the DSP bid-collection window) is 100ms and must not change — this is separate from the 150ms end-to-end ad-request SLO mentioned in the description. The adaptive per-DSP timeout mechanism (HdrHistogram-based, P95 signal, ≥100 sample cold start) is already implemented and running in production. Your proposal must configure BidStream as a new participant within this existing mechanism — not design a new protocol-level timeout scheme. BidStream starts at T_global=100ms (cold start); describe exactly when adaptive mode activates and what per-DSP timeout value is used once the histogram is warm. Revenue impact of timed-out BidStream bids must be tracked via a dedicated metric.

checks.text: Does the configuration keep the global RTB timeout at or below 100ms?
Does the adaptive timeout activation require at least 100 samples before switching from the static fallback?
Does the proposal use per-DSP P95 (not P99 or mean) as the adaptive timeout signal?

should_prune.description: Proposal that raises the global RTB timeout to 350ms to accommodate BidStream P99
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-003 — T_global is 100ms and per-DSP timeouts must not exceed T_global

should_prune.description: Proposal that sets BidStream timeout based on fewer than 100 samples
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-003 — adaptive timeout must not activate until 100 samples collected

should_prune.description: Proposal that stores DSP latency histograms in Redis for cross-instance sharing
should_prune.violates: CONSTRAINT-003 — histogram data must be in-process per-instance, not external store

should_pass: Proposal that starts BidStream at T_global=100ms, builds histogram over first 100 responses, activates adaptive timeout after cold start, and instruments timeout_revenue_loss metric for BidStream specifically
